titleStmt.title: SII 5.638: original edition by H. Krishna Sastri
No. 638. (A.R. No. 78-B of 1895). This inscription is engraved below No. 78-A. ON THE SAME WALL.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
2 10 2 Āvatu ḷ 3 100 7 10 6 kuravai nāraṇakkiramavittarāna maturān=ta ka brahma mārāyar mārāciyār Irāman coḻa
kulacun=ta riyār Uṭaiyār vaicciyanāta de varkkuk kuṭutta tan=ta kkāl paḷḷikkaṭṭil 1- m Irupaṟai nilaikkaṇṇā
ṭi Aṭiyum kāmpum tarāvuṭpaṭa nilamThis symbol which usually denotes nilam stands for niṟai in this inscription. 100 10 7 palam kaṇṇāṭi 1- m tarākkāl 1 nilam 3 10 6 palam 3 I-c cāṇiḷa
ttu neytāṅkiyum Aṭiyum Uṭpaṭa pittaḷai viḷakku 1 nilam 100 palam ciyārāvamuta(tā)n Ivai koṭuvan=tā ṉ
Ittirumaḻuvāṭi Uṭaiyār tevarkanmi kaḷom Immārāyan coḻakulacun=ta riyey Uṭaiyārk
ku ceyta nivan=ta ṅkaḷukkut tan sva miṭṭut tiruttina nilamāvatu Ittevar tevatānam tiruma
ḻuvāṭiyil cempiyanmātevip pereri rājarā jan tūmpilniṉṟu kiḻakku nokkip pon=ta vāy
kkālil vaṭakarai Uṟṟa varampukku teṟkum Ivvāykkālukkey kiḻakkum vaṭakkum meṟkumāka naṭuvu
paṭṭa nilam Araiye yiraṇṭumāvināl Āṇṭuvarai cempiyan māteviyennum marakkālāl la
ṭaippaṭi nellu Aṟupatin kalamum koṇṭu Uṭaiyār niccalpaṭi
Āṭiyaruḷum pāl nānāḻikku nelluk kuṟuṇi nānāḻiyum niccaṟpaṭi cārttiyaruḷum tumpaip pū
t tiruppaḷḷittāmam Irunāḻikku nellu nānāḻiyum Āka Orunāḷaikku nellupa patakkāka Āṇṭu
varai nellu Aṟupatin kalam Āka Ippaṭi Innivan=ta ṅkaḷ yāṇṭu 3 10Āvatu mutal can=ti rātittavaṟ ce
luttuvatānom tevarkanmi kaḷom Innilattukku tiruvācalil pon=ta kuṭimaiyum Ūrkaḻañcu kumara
kaccāṇamum veḷḷānveṭṭiyum Uḷḷiṭṭu Epperppaṭṭa Iṟaiyum Ivaṉ tan=ta kācu Aimpatu miṭṭu ce
lluka koṇṭu Ivvūr cempiyanmātevippereri kallik karaiyaṭṭik koṇṭamaiyil Innellukkāka
vey can=ti rātittavaṟ kāṭṭappeṟātomānom tevar kanmi kaḷom Itu patmāheśarar ra kṣai .
Digital edition of SII 5.638 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Renato Dávalos.
